title: How Does LA Count Election Ballots? It Begins With a Dog Sniff.
description: Counting ballots in California is a complex and lengthy undertaking. In Los Angeles, the choreographed process includes canines, humans and machines.
author: Orlando Mayorquín

title: Why Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass Has to Fight for Her Job
description: Ms. Bass, a Democrat, has had a roller-coaster first term. She now faces the first runoff since 2005 for an incumbent mayor in the city.
author: Jill Cowan

title: Voters in California City Favor Permanent Ban on Data Centers
description: Monterey Park, outside Los Angeles, appeared to become the first U.S. city where voters passed such a measure.
author: Billy Witz

title: Republicans Begin to Test the Limits of Trump’s Power by Flexing Their Own
description: The president’s unilateral and retributive style of governing is starting to hit a wall in both chambers of Congress.
author: Katie Rogers

title: House Advances New Sanctions on Russia and Aid to Ukraine
description: After G.O.P. leaders blocked additional aid to Ukraine, six Republicans and an independent joined Democrats to force the measure to the floor against the wishes of the speaker.
author: Robert Jimison

title: Massie, 3 Other House Republicans Broke From Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
description: Four Republicans from different ideological factions crossed party lines to vote with Democrats in favor of reining in the president’s power to wage war unilaterally.
author: Robert Jimison

title: Louisiana ICE Facility Mistreated Immigrants, Federal Investigators Say
description: A report by the Department of Homeland Security’s internal watchdog described officers putting one man in a chokehold and stabbing another with a pen.
author: Hamed Aleaziz and Nicholas Nehamas

title: As Ebola Outbreak Widens, Trump Has Yet to Outline a Plan
description: Despite long-established procedures for bringing Americans home for monitoring and treatment, the Trump administration has not said that it will allow those at risk of Ebola back into the country.
author: Apoorva Mandavilli

title: The Narrow Path to a Democratic Senate Runs Through Very Red States
description: Democrats cheer there is a way, even as new worries emerge over whether Graham Platner can flip a Maine seat. Republicans remain confident they will prevail in Texas, Iowa and Alaska.
author: Shane Goldmacher and Reid J. Epstein

title: G.O.P. Revives Immigration Bill, Weighing Ban on Trump’s Fund
description: Several Republicans suggested they would insist on adding a measure to bar the president from creating a fund to pay people who claim to be victims of government persecution.
author: Annie Karni

title: Obscure Group With Trump Ties Plans to Route Funds to His Allies for Legal Fights
description: A nonprofit called the Lawfare Defense Fund has raised $36 million to support those who consider themselves unjust victims of the legal system with grants to pay for litigation.
author: Theodore Schleifer and Ken Bensinger

title: ‘I Love It’: Trump Is Still in Favor of $1.8 Billion Payout Fund
description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, said on Tuesday that the administration was “not moving forward with the fund, period,” after the plan drew bipartisan backlash.
author: Luke Broadwater

title: In Alabama Ruling, Supreme Court Signals Limited Role for Federal Courts in Redistricting Fights
description: In an emergency ruling on Tuesday night, the court’s conservative majority gave a first glimpse into congressional district battles under a weakened Voting Rights Act.
author: Abbie VanSickle

title: Trump Signs Executive Order Removing Job Protections From Federal Workers
description: The order is a response to resistance during the first Trump administration, when senior career government officials routinely pushed back against policies that appeared to exceed legal boundaries.
author: Eileen Sullivan

title: Park Service Awards No-Bid Contract to Cover Bridge Statues in Gold
description: Originally estimated to cost $2.4 million, the government will now spend $5 million to restore the bronzes in time for Independence Day.
author: David A. Fahrenthold

title: Eric Swalwell Won More Than 18,000 Votes Despite Suspending His Campaign
description: The former House member withdrew from the California governor’s race amid allegations of sexual assault.
author: Tim Balk

title: New Intelligence Chief’s Expertise: Pursuing Trump’s Enemies
description: Tulsi Gabbard’s replacement as the director of national intelligence has no known national security experience.
author: Julian E. Barnes

title: Bessent Calls Clash With Bill Pulte a ‘Locker Room’ Fight
description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said he had an argument with the housing finance director last year but now supports his appointment as acting director of national intelligence.
author: Alan Rappeport

title: ‘Infuriated’ Former Judges Take on Trump
description: Critics say it is unseemly for retired judges to trade on the prestige of their former positions.
author: Adam Liptak

title: In Apparent Reversal, Mullin Says Abrego Garcia Could Be Deported to Costa Rica
description: Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia has long said he would willingly go to Costa Rica. His lawyers sent the new homeland security secretary’s comments to the judge handling his deportation case.
author: Alan Feuer
